page: Amazon Web Services m8ib.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server instance powered by Intel processors. It features an x86_64 architecture with 192 virtual CPUs mapped to 96 physical cores, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz with dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning storage must be attached externally. It provides a high-capacity network interface with a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps, which supports high-throughput data transfers and distributed workloads.

Given its bare-metal nature and high core count, the m8ib.metal-48xl is designed for workloads requiring direct hardware access, high-performance networking, and substantial memory capacity. It is suitable for large-scale general-purpose applications, enterprise databases, and containerized microservices that benefit from dedicated physical resources without virtualization overhead.
description: Amazon Web Services m8ib.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server instance featuring 192 vCPUs across 96 physical Intel cores with a 3.9 GHz clock speed. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 768.0 GB of dedicated system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on external storage solutions. It features a high-performance network interface delivering a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. This bare-metal configuration is designed for enterprise workloads, large-scale databases, and containerized environments requiring direct hardware access, high-speed networking, and substantial compute density without virtualization overhead.
